page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.3xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 24 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX-512F, and AVX-512 VNNI vector extensions. System memory consists of 48.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on external EBS storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m5zn.3xlarge demonstrates average performance across most compute and memory metrics. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1871.89, while the multi-core performance reaches 14800.7. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 824.5 GB/sec and a write large block speed of 45.49 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, achieving 31.64 GB/sec, which falls into the top 25% of tested configurations. Conversely, the instance shows weaker performance in medium-scale LLM inference, processing prompts for a 7B model at 20.52 tokens/sec, though it maintains an average text generation speed of 9.79 tokens/sec for the same model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5zn.3xlarge offers a high-frequency CPU profile within a general-purpose memory-to-core ratio, making it suitable for workloads that require high single-threaded compute performance without the massive memory footprints of memory-optimized classes. The lack of local NVMe storage means storage-heavy workloads will incur additional EBS costs, representing a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is suited for applications such as static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from high clock speeds and dedicated CPU allocation.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.3xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance powered by a dedicated 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 12 vCPUs and 48.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate average performance across most categories, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1544.0 and multi-core score of 7676.0, alongside a strong single-threaded gzip compression speed of 31.64 GB/sec. However, LLM prompt processing for a 7B model is relatively weak at 20.52 tokens/sec. This instance is suited for high-frequency general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and database operations where high clock speed is required but local storage is not a necessity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 12 vCPUs, and 48 GB DDR4 RAM, delivering strong single-threaded compression and 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance with a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 12 vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency, general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and optimized network performance for compute-sensitive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor operating at 4.5 GHz
12 vCPUs and 48.0 GB of DDR4 RAM with a 1:4 ratio
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 31.64 GB/sec
No local storage or integrated GPU hardware included
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
